relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Children are called into right relation with parents in the Lord.
Honor of father and mother carries promise.
The promise seeks wellbeing and long life upon earth.
Parents must not provoke children but nourish them in the Lord’s formation.
Ancient enslaved hearers are addressed inside their constrained world.
Service is not eye-service for human approval but soul-deep before Messiah.
Goodwill serves the Lord beyond human masters.
The Lord sees good done by enslaved and free alike.
Masters are told to give up threats and remember the one Master without partiality.
Be empowered in the Lord’s strength.
Put on Divine armor to stand against adversarial schemes.
The struggle is not against blood and flesh, but against harmful powers and dark structures.
The armor enables standing in the harmful day.
Truth girds the body; justice guards the heart.
Feet are readied by the good news of peace.
Trust shields against flaming arrows of harm.
Salvation/healing covers the head; Spirit’s sword is Divine word.
Prayer in Spirit keeps the body awake for all holy ones.
Paul asks for bold speech to reveal the mystery of the good news.
Even chained, he is ambassador of the mystery.
Tychicus will make Paul’s situation known.
He is sent to comfort and encourage hearts.
Peace, love, and trust come from the Father and Lord Jesus Messiah.
Grace rests with all who love the Lord Jesus in incorruptibility.

contemplative_note: Ephesians 6 closes with resilience, not aggression. The armor is truth, justice, peace, trust, salvation/healing, Spirit-word, and prayer against harmful powers — never violence toward people.
